metadata.title: SOC 2 Type II Trust Services Criteria

controls.title: COSO Principle 1: Demonstrates Commitment to Integrity and Ethical Values

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values through a code of conduct, ethics training, and management oversight that sets the tone at the top for internal controls.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Establish and distribute a code of conduct with annual employee acknowledgment tracking.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store the code of conduct in a versioned S3 bucket: aws s3 cp code-of-conduct.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/policies/code-of-conduct.pdf
- Enable versioning on the bucket: aws s3api put-bucket-versioning --bucket <policy-bucket> --versioning-configuration Status=Enabled
- Use AWS WorkDocs or an HRIS system to track employee acknowledgments
- Create an EventBridge scheduled rule to trigger annual re-acknowledgment reminders

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify a code of conduct or ethics policy exists, is current, and has been acknowledged by all personnel

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement annual ethics training with completion tracking via LMS or HR system.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store training completion records in DynamoDB: aws dynamodb put-item --table-name TrainingRecords --item '{"employee_id":{"S":"<id>"},"training":{"S":"ethics"},"completed_date":{"S":"<date>"}}'
- Query incomplete trainings: aws dynamodb scan --table-name TrainingRecords --filter-expression 'completed_date < :cutoff' --expression-attribute-values '{":cutoff":{"S":"<one-year-ago>"}}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify ethics training is completed annually by all employees

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The board of directors demonstrates independence from management and exercises oversight of the development and performance of internal controls through governance structures such as audit committees.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Maintain a board or audit committee charter documenting oversight responsibilities and meeting cadence.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store governance documents in versioned S3: aws s3 cp audit-committee-charter.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/governance/audit-committee-charter.pdf
- Tag with review date: aws s3api put-object-tagging --bucket <policy-bucket> --key governance/audit-committee-charter.pdf --tagging 'TagSet=[{Key=ReviewDate,Value=<date>}]'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify board or audit committee charter exists with defined oversight responsibilities

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Document quarterly board and audit committee meetings with minutes stored centrally.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Upload meeting minutes: aws s3 cp meeting-minutes-Q1.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/governance/minutes/
- List available minutes: aws s3 ls s3://<policy-bucket>/governance/minutes/ --recursive

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify regular board or audit committee meetings are held and documented

controls.title: COSO Principle 3: Establishes Structure, Authority, and Responsibility

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Management establishes, with board oversight, structures, reporting lines, and appropriate authorities and responsibilities in the pursuit of objectives.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Maintain an up-to-date organizational chart with clearly defined security roles and reporting lines.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store org chart in S3: aws s3 cp org-chart.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/governance/org-chart.pdf
- Use AWS IAM to mirror organizational structure with groups: aws iam create-group --group-name SecurityTeam
- List IAM groups to verify structure: aws iam list-groups --query 'Groups[*].GroupName'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify organizational chart with defined reporting lines and security responsibilities exists

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Create and maintain a RACI matrix for security responsibilities.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store RACI matrix: aws s3 cp raci-matrix.xlsx s3://<policy-bucket>/governance/raci-matrix.xlsx
- Verify IAM roles match documented responsibilities: aws iam list-roles --query 'Roles[*].[RoleName,Description]'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify roles and responsibilities for security functions are documented and assigned

controls.title: COSO Principle 4: Demonstrates Commitment to Competence

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity demonstrates a commitment to attract, develop, and retain competent individuals in alignment with objectives, including security awareness training and professional development programs.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement a security awareness training program with tracked completion and annual refresher.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Track training in DynamoDB: aws dynamodb create-table --table-name SecurityTraining --attribute-definitions AttributeName=employee_id,AttributeType=S --key-schema AttributeName=employee_id,KeyType=HASH --billing-mode PAY_PER_REQUEST
- Record completion: aws dynamodb put-item --table-name SecurityTraining --item '{"employee_id":{"S":"<id>"},"module":{"S":"security-awareness"},"completed":{"S":"<date>"},"score":{"N":"<score>"}}'
- Audit overdue trainings: aws dynamodb scan --table-name SecurityTraining --filter-expression 'completed < :cutoff' --expression-attribute-values '{":cutoff":{"S":"<one-year-ago>"}}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify security awareness training program exists and completion records are maintained

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Ensure all job descriptions include relevant security responsibilities and are stored centrally.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store job descriptions: aws s3 sync ./job-descriptions/ s3://<policy-bucket>/hr/job-descriptions/
- List stored descriptions: aws s3 ls s3://<policy-bucket>/hr/job-descriptions/

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify job descriptions include security responsibilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity holds individuals accountable for their internal control responsibilities in the pursuit of objectives through performance evaluations, disciplinary processes, and escalation procedures.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Include security accountability metrics in performance evaluations and document the process.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store performance review policy: aws s3 cp performance-review-policy.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/hr/performance-review-policy.pdf
- Tag with effective date: aws s3api put-object-tagging --bucket <policy-bucket> --key hr/performance-review-policy.pdf --tagging 'TagSet=[{Key=EffectiveDate,Value=<date>}]'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify performance evaluation process includes security accountability metrics

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Document and maintain a disciplinary action policy for security violations.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store disciplinary policy: aws s3 cp disciplinary-policy.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/hr/disciplinary-policy.pdf
- Verify policy exists: aws s3api head-object --bucket <policy-bucket> --key hr/disciplinary-policy.pdf

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify disciplinary process for security policy violations is documented

controls.title: COSO Principle 13: Obtains or Generates Relevant Information

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity obtains or generates and uses relevant, quality information to support the functioning of internal controls, including information about security events, incidents, and system changes.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable centralized logging via CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and Security Hub to capture security-relevant information.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable CloudTrail: aws cloudtrail create-trail --name soc2-trail --s3-bucket-name <bucket> --is-multi-region-trail --enable-log-file-validation
- Start logging: aws cloudtrail start-logging --name soc2-trail
- Enable Security Hub: aws securityhub enable-security-hub --enable-default-standards
- Create CloudWatch log group: aws logs create-log-group --log-group-name /soc2/security-events --retention-in-days 365
- Verify trail status: aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name soc2-trail

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify centralized logging and monitoring captures relevant security information

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable CloudTrail log file validation and S3 object lock for log integrity.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Verify log validation: aws cloudtrail get-trail --name soc2-trail --query 'Trail.LogFileValidationEnabled'
- Enable S3 object lock on log bucket: aws s3api put-object-lock-configuration --bucket <log-bucket> --object-lock-configuration '{"ObjectLockEnabled":"Enabled","Rule":{"DefaultRetention":{"Mode":"COMPLIANCE","Days":365}}}'
- Validate log digest: aws cloudtrail validate-logs --trail-arn <trail-arn> --start-time <start>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify information quality controls are in place for security data

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity internally communicates information, including objectives and responsibilities for internal control, necessary to support the functioning of internal controls.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Establish internal communication channels for security policies, responsibilities, and incident notifications.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create SNS topic for internal security communications: aws sns create-topic --name security-announcements
- Subscribe stakeholders: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <topic-arn> --protocol email --notification-endpoint <email>
- Store communication policy: aws s3 cp internal-comms-policy.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/policies/internal-comms-policy.pdf

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify internal communication of security policies, responsibilities, and incidents

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Track employee acknowledgment of security policies with dated records.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Record acknowledgments: aws dynamodb put-item --table-name PolicyAcknowledgments --item '{"employee_id":{"S":"<id>"},"policy":{"S":"information-security"},"acknowledged_date":{"S":"<date>"}}'
- Audit missing acknowledgments: aws dynamodb scan --table-name PolicyAcknowledgments --filter-expression 'acknowledged_date < :cutoff' --expression-attribute-values '{":cutoff":{"S":"<one-year-ago>"}}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify security policy acknowledgment process exists for employees

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity communicates with external parties regarding matters affecting the functioning of internal controls, including commitments, system descriptions, and security incident notifications.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Document external communication procedures for security incidents, breaches, and regulatory notifications.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store incident communication plan: aws s3 cp external-comms-plan.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/policies/external-comms-plan.pdf
- Create SNS topic for external notifications: aws sns create-topic --name security-external-notifications
- Configure SES for incident notification emails: aws ses verify-email-identity --email-address security@<domain>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify external communication processes for security incidents and obligations

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity specifies objectives with sufficient clarity to enable the identification and assessment of risks relating to objectives, including security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Document and maintain security objectives that align with business goals and Trust Services Criteria.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store security objectives document: aws s3 cp security-objectives.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/governance/security-objectives.pdf
- Tag with review period: aws s3api put-object-tagging --bucket <policy-bucket> --key governance/security-objectives.pdf --tagging 'TagSet=[{Key=ReviewDate,Value=<date>},{Key=Owner,Value=CISO}]'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify documented security objectives aligned with business goals

controls.title: COSO Principle 7: Identifies and Analyzes Risk

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity identifies risks to the achievement of its objectives across the entity and analyzes risks as a basis for determining how the risks should be managed, including threats and vulnerabilities.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Conduct annual risk assessments and maintain a risk register with identified threats and vulnerabilities.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store risk register: aws s3 cp risk-register.xlsx s3://<policy-bucket>/risk/risk-register.xlsx
- Enable AWS Inspector for vulnerability assessment: aws inspector2 enable --resource-types EC2 ECR LAMBDA
- List Inspector findings: aws inspector2 list-findings --filter-criteria '{"findingStatus":[{"comparison":"EQUALS","value":"ACTIVE"}]}'
- Enable Trusted Advisor checks: aws support describe-trusted-advisor-checks --language en

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify a formal risk assessment has been performed and documented

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable continuous vulnerability scanning via AWS Inspector and review findings weekly.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Verify Inspector is enabled: aws inspector2 batch-get-account-status
- List active findings: aws inspector2 list-findings --filter-criteria '{"severity":[{"comparison":"EQUALS","value":"HIGH"},{"comparison":"EQUALS","value":"CRITICAL"}]}'
- Export findings to S3: aws inspector2 create-findings-report --report-format CSV --s3-destination '{"bucketName":"<bucket>","keyPrefix":"inspector-reports/"}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify vulnerability scanning is performed regularly

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity considers the potential for fraud in assessing risks to the achievement of objectives, including unauthorized access, data manipulation, and misappropriation of assets.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Include fraud risk scenarios in the risk register and enable anomaly detection controls.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store fraud risk assessment: aws s3 cp fraud-risk-assessment.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/risk/fraud-risk-assessment.pdf
- Enable GuardDuty for anomaly detection: aws guardduty create-detector --enable --finding-publishing-frequency FIFTEEN_MINUTES
- Enable CloudTrail Insights: aws cloudtrail put-insight-selectors --trail-name soc2-trail --insight-selectors '[{"InsightType":"ApiCallRateInsight"},{"InsightType":"ApiErrorRateInsight"}]'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify fraud risk assessment is performed and documented

controls.title: COSO Principle 9: Identifies and Analyzes Significant Change

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity identifies and assesses changes that could significantly impact the system of internal controls, including infrastructure changes, personnel changes, and regulatory changes.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Document change management procedures that include impact assessment for significant changes.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store change management policy: aws s3 cp change-management-policy.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/policies/change-management-policy.pdf
- Enable AWS Config to track configuration changes: aws configservice put-configuration-recorder --configuration-recorder name=default,roleARN=<role-arn> --recording-group allSupported=true
- Start recorder: aws configservice start-configuration-recorder --configuration-recorder-name default

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify change impact assessment process is documented and followed

controls.title: COSO Principle 16: Selects, Develops, and Performs Ongoing and Separate Evaluations

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity selects, develops, and performs ongoing and/or separate evaluations to ascertain whether the components of internal control are present and functioning, including continuous monitoring and periodic assessments.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement continuous monitoring via AWS Config rules, Security Hub, and CloudWatch alarms.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable Security Hub with CIS benchmark: aws securityhub enable-security-hub --enable-default-standards
- Create Config rules for compliance: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule '{"ConfigRuleName":"iam-password-policy","Source":{"Owner":"AWS","SourceIdentifier":"IAM_PASSWORD_POLICY"}}'
- Create CloudWatch alarm for unauthorized API calls: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name unauthorized-api-calls --metric-name UnauthorizedAttemptCount --namespace CloudTrailMetrics --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 5 --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold --alarm-actions <sns-arn>
- Verify Security Hub compliance: aws securityhub get-findings --filters '{"ComplianceStatus":[{"Value":"FAILED","Comparison":"EQUALS"}]}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify continuous monitoring controls are implemented and operational

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Schedule and document periodic internal audits of security controls.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store audit reports: aws s3 cp internal-audit-report.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/audits/internal-audit-<date>.pdf
- Run AWS Audit Manager assessment: aws auditmanager create-assessment --name soc2-assessment --framework-id <framework-id> --assessment-reports-destination '{"destinationType":"S3","destination":"s3://<bucket>/audit-reports/"}' --roles '[{"roleType":"PROCESS_OWNER","roleArn":"<role-arn>"}]' --scope '{"awsAccounts":[{"id":"<account-id>"}]}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify periodic internal assessments or audits are performed

controls.title: COSO Principle 17: Evaluates and Communicates Deficiencies

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity evaluates and communicates internal control deficiencies in a timely manner to those parties responsible for taking corrective action, including senior management and the board of directors.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Track control deficiencies in a centralized system with assigned owners and remediation timelines.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Export Security Hub findings as deficiency list: aws securityhub get-findings --filters '{"ComplianceStatus":[{"Value":"FAILED","Comparison":"EQUALS"}],"SeverityLabel":[{"Value":"HIGH","Comparison":"EQUALS"},{"Value":"CRITICAL","Comparison":"EQUALS"}]}'
- Store deficiency tracking spreadsheet: aws s3 cp deficiency-tracker.xlsx s3://<policy-bucket>/risk/deficiency-tracker.xlsx
- Set up SNS notification for new critical findings: aws events put-rule --name critical-deficiency-alert --event-pattern '{"source":["aws.securityhub"],"detail":{"findings":{"Severity":{"Label":["CRITICAL","HIGH"]}}}}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify deficiency tracking and remediation process is documented and followed

controls.title: COSO Principle 10: Selects and Develops Control Activities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity selects and develops control activities that contribute to the mitigation of risks to the achievement of objectives to acceptable levels, including IT general controls and application controls.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Document IT general controls and map them to risks in the risk register.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store ITGC documentation: aws s3 cp itgc-control-matrix.xlsx s3://<policy-bucket>/controls/itgc-control-matrix.xlsx
- Enable AWS Config conformance packs: aws configservice put-conformance-pack --conformance-pack-name Operational-Best-Practices-for-SOC2 --template-s3-uri s3://aws-config-conformance-pack-templates/Operational-Best-Practices-for-SOC2.yaml

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify IT general controls are documented and mapped to identified risks

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement automated controls using AWS Config rules and remediation actions.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List active Config rules: aws configservice describe-config-rules --query 'ConfigRules[*].[ConfigRuleName,ConfigRuleState]'
- Create auto-remediation: aws configservice put-remediation-configurations --remediation-configurations '[{"ConfigRuleName":"<rule>","TargetType":"SSM_DOCUMENT","TargetId":"<ssm-doc>","Automatic":true}]'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify automated controls are implemented where feasible

controls.title: COSO Principle 11: Selects and Develops General Controls over Technology

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity selects and develops general control activities over technology to support the achievement of objectives, including access management, change management, and operations controls.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement technology general controls across access management, change management, and operations.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Verify IAM password policy: aws iam get-account-password-policy
- Enforce strong password policy: aws iam update-account-password-policy --minimum-password-length 14 --require-symbols --require-numbers --require-uppercase-characters --require-lowercase-characters --max-password-age 90 --password-reuse-prevention 24
- Enable AWS Config rules for technology controls: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule '{"ConfigRuleName":"iam-password-policy","Source":{"Owner":"AWS","SourceIdentifier":"IAM_PASSWORD_POLICY"}}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify technology controls cover access management, change management, and operations

controls.title: COSO Principle 12: Deploys Through Policies and Procedures

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity deploys control activities through policies that establish what is expected and procedures that put policies into action, with regular review and updates.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Maintain a policy library with version control, approval records, and annual review dates.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List all policies in S3: aws s3 ls s3://<policy-bucket>/policies/ --recursive
- Check policy metadata for review dates: aws s3api get-object-tagging --bucket <policy-bucket> --key policies/<policy-name>
- Enable S3 versioning for policy change tracking: aws s3api put-bucket-versioning --bucket <policy-bucket> --versioning-configuration Status=Enabled

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify security policies are documented, approved, and reviewed on a regular cadence

controls.title: Logical Access Security Software, Infrastructure, and Architectures

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement centralized identity management with SSO and federated access.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable AWS IAM Identity Center (SSO): aws sso-admin list-instances
- List permission sets: aws sso-admin list-permission-sets --instance-arn <instance-arn>
- Verify SAML providers: aws iam list-saml-providers
- Ensure no console passwords for service accounts: aws iam get-credential-report

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose:
- Verify Entra ID SSO apps: az ad app list --query '[].{Name:displayName,SignOnUrl:web.homePageUrl}'
- List conditional access policies: az rest --method get --url 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/conditionalAccess/policies'

parts.name: remediation-gcp
parts.prose:
- List identity-aware proxy settings: gcloud iap web get-iam-policy
- Verify workspace SSO: gcloud identity groups list

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify identity provider and SSO are configured for centralized authentication

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enforce MFA for all IAM users and console access.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Generate credential report: aws iam generate-credential-report && sleep 5 && aws iam get-credential-report --output text --query Content | base64 -d
- Identify users without MFA and enable it: aws iam create-virtual-mfa-device --virtual-mfa-device-name <user>-mfa --outfile qr.png --bootstrap-method QRCodePNG
- Enable MFA device: aws iam enable-mfa-device --user-name <user> --serial-number <mfa-arn> --authentication-code1 <code1> --authentication-code2 <code2>
- Create Config rule: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule '{"ConfigRuleName":"iam-user-mfa-enabled","Source":{"Owner":"AWS","SourceIdentifier":"IAM_USER_MFA_ENABLED"}}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify MFA is enforced for all user access

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Prior to issuing system credentials and granting system access, the entity registers and authorizes new internal and external users whose access is administered by the entity.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement an access request and approval workflow for user provisioning.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List all IAM users: aws iam list-users --query 'Users[*].[UserName,CreateDate]' --output table
- Check for users created without tagging (approval evidence): aws iam list-user-tags --user-name <user>
- Tag users with approval ticket: aws iam tag-user --user-name <user> --tags Key=ApprovalTicket,Value=<ticket-id>
- Create SCP to require tags on user creation: aws organizations create-policy --name require-approval-tag --type SERVICE_CONTROL_POLICY --content '{"Version":"2012-10-17","Statement":[]}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify user provisioning process requires approval prior to access grant

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Perform quarterly access reviews and document findings and remediation actions.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Generate credential report for review: aws iam generate-credential-report && sleep 5 && aws iam get-credential-report --output text --query Content | base64 -d > access-review.csv
- List all user policies: for user in $(aws iam list-users --query 'Users[*].UserName' --output text); do echo "=== $user ==="; aws iam list-attached-user-policies --user-name $user; done
- Identify stale access keys (>90 days): aws iam get-credential-report --output text --query Content | base64 -d | awk -F, '$10 != "N/A" && $10 < "<90-days-ago>"'
- Store access review: aws s3 cp access-review.csv s3://<policy-bucket>/audits/access-reviews/access-review-<date>.csv

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify access reviews are performed periodically

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access to data, software, functions, and other protected information assets based on roles, responsibilities, or the system design and changes, giving consideration to the concepts of least privilege and segregation of duties.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement RBAC using IAM groups and roles instead of direct user policy attachments.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Identify users with inline or directly attached policies: aws iam list-users --query 'Users[*].UserName' --output text | xargs -I{} sh -c 'echo "User: {}"; aws iam list-user-policies --user-name {}; aws iam list-attached-user-policies --user-name {}'
- Create IAM groups for roles: aws iam create-group --group-name Developers && aws iam create-group --group-name Operators && aws iam create-group --group-name ReadOnly
- Attach scoped policies to groups: aws iam attach-group-policy --group-name ReadOnly --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/ReadOnlyAccess
- Move users from direct policies to groups: aws iam add-user-to-group --user-name <user> --group-name <group>
- Use IAM Access Analyzer to generate least-privilege policies: aws accessanalyzer start-policy-generation --policy-generation-details '{"principalArn":"<user-arn>"}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify role-based access control (RBAC) is implemented

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enforce segregation of duties by separating admin, developer, and audit roles.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Verify no user has both admin and audit roles: aws iam list-groups-for-user --user-name <user>
- Create SCP to prevent role conflicts: aws organizations create-policy --name sod-policy --type SERVICE_CONTROL_POLICY --content <policy-json>
- Use permission boundaries: aws iam put-user-permissions-boundary --user-name <user> --permissions-boundary arn:aws:iam::<account>:policy/DeveloperBoundary

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify segregation of duties is enforced for critical functions

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity restricts physical access to facilities and protected information assets to authorized personnel to meet the entity's objectives.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Document physical security controls and validate cloud provider SOC 2 compliance for data centers.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Download AWS SOC 2 report via Artifact: aws artifact get-report-metadata (or access via console)
- Store physical security policy: aws s3 cp physical-security-policy.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/policies/physical-security-policy.pdf
- Document data center locations: aws ec2 describe-regions --query 'Regions[*].RegionName'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify physical access controls are documented and cloud provider compliance is validated

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity discontinues logical and physical protections over physical assets only after the ability to read or recover data and software from those assets has been diminished and is no longer required to meet the entity's objectives.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement data disposal procedures including encryption key destruction and media sanitization.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store data disposal policy: aws s3 cp data-disposal-policy.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/policies/data-disposal-policy.pdf
- Verify EBS volumes are encrypted: aws ec2 describe-volumes --query 'Volumes[?!Encrypted].[VolumeId,State]'
- Schedule KMS key deletion for disposed data: aws kms schedule-key-deletion --key-id <key-id> --pending-window-in-days 7
- Verify S3 bucket lifecycle rules for data expiration: aws s3api get-bucket-lifecycle-configuration --bucket <bucket>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify data disposal and media sanitization procedures are documented and followed

controls.title: Security Measures Against Threats Outside System Boundaries

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries, including firewalls, intrusion detection, and DDoS protection.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement WAF, Shield, and network firewalls to protect against external threats.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List WAF web ACLs: aws wafv2 list-web-acls --scope REGIONAL
- Create WAF web ACL: aws wafv2 create-web-acl --name soc2-waf --scope REGIONAL --default-action '{"Block":{}}' --visibility-config '{"SampledRequestsEnabled":true,"CloudWatchMetricsEnabled":true,"MetricName":"soc2-waf"}' --rules '[]'
- Enable Shield Advanced: aws shield create-subscription
- Enable GuardDuty: aws guardduty create-detector --enable
- Check for open security groups: aws ec2 describe-security-groups --filters Name=ip-permission.cidr,Values=0.0.0.0/0 --query 'SecurityGroups[*].[GroupId,GroupName]'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose:
- List WAF policies: az network application-gateway waf-policy list
- Enable DDoS protection: az network ddos-protection create --name ddos-plan --resource-group <rg>

parts.name: remediation-gcp
parts.prose:
- List Cloud Armor policies: gcloud compute security-policies list
- Create security policy: gcloud compute security-policies create soc2-policy

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify network perimeter security controls are implemented

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable GuardDuty and Network Firewall for intrusion detection and prevention.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Verify GuardDuty is enabled: aws guardduty list-detectors
- Check GuardDuty findings: aws guardduty list-findings --detector-id <detector-id> --finding-criteria '{"Criterion":{"severity":{"Gte":7}}}'
- Create Network Firewall: aws network-firewall create-firewall --firewall-name soc2-firewall --vpc-id <vpc-id> --subnet-mappings '[{"SubnetId":"<subnet-id>"}]' --firewall-policy-arn <policy-arn>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify intrusion detection and prevention systems are active

controls.title: Restrict Data Transmission, Movement, and Removal

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity restricts the transmission, movement, and removal of information to authorized internal and external users and processes, and protects it during transmission to meet the entity's objectives.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enforce TLS/SSL for all data in transit using ACM certificates and security policies.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List ACM certificates: aws acm list-certificates --query 'CertificateSummaryList[*].[DomainName,Status]'
- Verify ALB uses HTTPS: aws elbv2 describe-listeners --load-balancer-arn <alb-arn> --query 'Listeners[*].[Protocol,SslPolicy]'
- Enforce S3 bucket policy requiring TLS: aws s3api put-bucket-policy --bucket <bucket> --policy '{"Version":"2012-10-17","Statement":[{"Effect":"Deny","Principal":"*","Action":"s3:*","Resource":["arn:aws:s3:::<bucket>","arn:aws:s3:::<bucket>/*"],"Condition":{"Bool":{"aws:SecureTransport":"false"}}}]}'
- Create Config rule: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule '{"ConfigRuleName":"s3-bucket-ssl-requests-only","Source":{"Owner":"AWS","SourceIdentifier":"S3_BUCKET_SSL_REQUESTS_ONLY"}}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify encryption in transit is enforced for all data transmissions

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement DLP controls using Macie, S3 Block Public Access, and VPC endpoints.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable Macie: aws macie2 enable-macie
- Create Macie classification job: aws macie2 create-classification-job --job-type SCHEDULED --s3-job-definition '{"bucketDefinitions":[{"accountId":"<account-id>","buckets":["<bucket>"]}]}' --schedule-frequency-expression 'cron(0 12 ? * MON *)' --name sensitive-data-scan
- Enable S3 Block Public Access: aws s3control put-public-access-block --account-id <account-id> --public-access-block-configuration BlockPublicAcls=true,IgnorePublicAcls=true,BlockPublicPolicy=true,RestrictPublicBuckets=true
- Create VPC endpoint for S3: aws ec2 create-vpc-endpoint --vpc-id <vpc-id> --service-name com.amazonaws.<region>.s3 --route-table-ids <rtb-id>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify data loss prevention controls restrict unauthorized data movement

controls.title: Prevention and Detection of Unauthorized or Malicious Software

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity implements controls to prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorized or malicious software to meet the entity's objectives.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Deploy GuardDuty malware protection and SSM for endpoint management.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable GuardDuty malware protection: aws guardduty update-detector --detector-id <id> --features '[{"Name":"EBS_MALWARE_PROTECTION","Status":"ENABLED"}]'
- Verify SSM agent on all instances: aws ssm describe-instance-information --query 'InstanceInformationList[*].[InstanceId,PingStatus,PlatformType]'
- Run SSM patch compliance scan: aws ssm start-automation-execution --document-name AWS-RunPatchBaseline --parameters '{"Operation":["Scan"]}'
- Check ECR image scan findings: aws ecr describe-image-scan-findings --repository-name <repo> --image-id imageTag=latest

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify malware protection and endpoint security controls are deployed

controls.title: Detection and Monitoring of Security Events

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: To meet its objectives, the entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to identify changes to configurations that result in the introduction of new vulnerabilities and susceptibilities to newly discovered vulnerabilities.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement security monitoring using GuardDuty, Security Hub, and CloudWatch alarms.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable GuardDuty: aws guardduty create-detector --enable --finding-publishing-frequency FIFTEEN_MINUTES
- Enable Security Hub: aws securityhub enable-security-hub --enable-default-standards
- Create CloudWatch alarm for root login: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name root-login --namespace CloudTrailMetrics --metric-name RootLoginEventCount --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --alarm-actions <sns-arn>
- Create metric filter for unauthorized API calls: aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name CloudTrail/logs --filter-name UnauthorizedAPICalls --filter-pattern '{($.errorCode="*UnauthorizedAccess*")||($.errorCode="AccessDenied*")}' --metric-transformations metricName=UnauthorizedAttemptCount,metricNamespace=CloudTrailMetrics,metricValue=1

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose:
- Enable Microsoft Defender for Cloud: az security pricing create --name VirtualMachines --tier Standard
- Enable Azure Sentinel: az sentinel alert-rule list --workspace-name <ws>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify continuous security monitoring and alerting is configured

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable AWS Config to detect and record configuration changes.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable Config recorder: aws configservice put-configuration-recorder --configuration-recorder name=default,roleARN=<role-arn> --recording-group allSupported=true
- Start recording: aws configservice start-configuration-recorder --configuration-recorder-name default
- Set delivery channel: aws configservice put-delivery-channel --delivery-channel '{"name":"default","s3BucketName":"<config-bucket>","snsTopicARN":"<sns-arn>","configSnapshotDeliveryProperties":{"deliveryFrequency":"Six_Hours"}}'
- Verify status: aws configservice describe-configuration-recorder-status

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify configuration change detection is enabled

controls.title: Monitoring System Components for Anomalous Behavior

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable anomaly detection using CloudWatch Anomaly Detection and GuardDuty.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable CloudWatch anomaly detection: aws cloudwatch put-anomaly-detector --namespace AWS/EC2 --metric-name CPUUtilization --stat Average
- Create anomaly alarm: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name cpu-anomaly --namespace AWS/EC2 --metric-name CPUUtilization --threshold-metric-id ad1 --comparison-operator LessThanLowerOrGreaterThanUpperThreshold --evaluation-periods 3 --alarm-actions <sns-arn> --metrics '[{"Id":"m1","MetricStat":{"Metric":{"Namespace":"AWS/EC2","MetricName":"CPUUtilization"},"Period":300,"Stat":"Average"}},{"Id":"ad1","Expression":"ANOMALY_DETECTION_BAND(m1,2)"}]'
- Review GuardDuty anomaly findings: aws guardduty list-findings --detector-id <id> --finding-criteria '{"Criterion":{"type":{"Eq":["UnauthorizedAccess:IAMUser/InstanceCredentialExfiltration.OutsideAWS"]}}}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify anomaly detection is enabled for system components

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity evaluates security events to determine whether they could or have resulted in a failure of the entity to meet its objectives (security incidents) and, if so, takes actions to prevent or address such failures.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Maintain an incident response plan with defined roles, escalation procedures, and annual testing.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store incident response plan: aws s3 cp incident-response-plan.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/policies/incident-response-plan.pdf
- Create incident response SNS topic: aws sns create-topic --name incident-response
- Subscribe on-call team: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <topic-arn> --protocol email --notification-endpoint oncall@<domain>
- Create EventBridge rule for critical events: aws events put-rule --name critical-security-events --event-pattern '{"source":["aws.guardduty"],"detail":{"severity":[{"numeric":[">",7]}]}}'
- Set rule target to SNS: aws events put-targets --rule critical-security-events --targets '[{"Id":"ir-notify","Arn":"<sns-topic-arn>"}]'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify incident response plan is documented, tested, and current

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Automate incident detection and triage using GuardDuty and EventBridge.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List GuardDuty findings by severity: aws guardduty list-findings --detector-id <id> --sort-criteria '{"AttributeName":"severity","OrderBy":"DESC"}'
- Create automated remediation via SSM: aws ssm create-document --name IsolateCompromisedInstance --document-type Automation --content '{"schemaVersion":"0.3","mainSteps":[{"name":"isolateInstance","action":"aws:changeInstanceState","inputs":{"InstanceIds":["{{InstanceId}}"],"DesiredState":"stopped"}}]}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify incident detection and triage procedures are automated

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity responds to identified security incidents by executing a defined incident response program to understand, contain, remediate, and communicate security incidents, as appropriate.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Document and test incident containment, eradication, and recovery procedures at least annually.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store IR playbooks: aws s3 sync ./playbooks/ s3://<policy-bucket>/incident-response/playbooks/
- Create isolation security group: aws ec2 create-security-group --group-name incident-isolation --description 'No inbound/outbound for incident isolation' --vpc-id <vpc-id>
- Revoke all rules from isolation SG: aws ec2 revoke-security-group-egress --group-id <sg-id> --ip-permissions '[{"IpProtocol":"-1","IpRanges":[{"CidrIp":"0.0.0.0/0"}]}]'
- Create forensic snapshot automation: aws ssm create-document --name CreateForensicSnapshot --document-type Automation --content <automation-doc>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify incident containment, eradication, and recovery procedures are defined and tested

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity identifies, develops, and implements activities to recover from identified security incidents and uses information from the incidents to learn and improve.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Conduct post-incident reviews within 72 hours and store lessons learned for continuous improvement.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store post-incident reports: aws s3 cp post-incident-report-<incident-id>.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/incident-response/post-incident/
- List historical incident reports: aws s3 ls s3://<policy-bucket>/incident-response/post-incident/
- Track improvement actions in DynamoDB: aws dynamodb put-item --table-name IncidentImprovements --item '{"incident_id":{"S":"<id>"},"improvement":{"S":"<action>"},"status":{"S":"open"},"due_date":{"S":"<date>"}}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify post-incident review (lessons learned) process is documented and followed

controls.title: Changes to Infrastructure, Data, Software, and Procedures

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures to meet its objectives.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement a formal change management process with approval, testing, and rollback procedures.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store change management policy: aws s3 cp change-management-policy.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/policies/change-management-policy.pdf
- Enable AWS Config for change tracking: aws configservice start-configuration-recorder --configuration-recorder-name default
- Use CodePipeline for approved deployments: aws codepipeline list-pipelines
- Add manual approval stage: aws codepipeline update-pipeline --pipeline '{"name":"<pipeline>","stages":[{"name":"Approval","actions":[{"name":"ManualApproval","actionTypeId":{"category":"Approval","owner":"AWS","provider":"Manual","version":"1"},"configuration":{"NotificationArn":"<sns-arn>"}}]}]}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify change management process with documented approval workflows

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement pre-production testing stages in CI/CD pipelines.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List CodeBuild projects: aws codebuild list-projects
- Verify test stage in pipeline: aws codepipeline get-pipeline --name <pipeline> --query 'pipeline.stages[?name==`Test`]'
- Create CodeBuild test project: aws codebuild create-project --name unit-tests --source '{"type":"CODEPIPELINE"}' --artifacts '{"type":"CODEPIPELINE"}' --environment '{"type":"LINUX_CONTAINER","image":"aws/codebuild/standard:7.0","computeType":"BUILD_GENERAL1_SMALL"}'
- Verify separate staging environment: aws ec2 describe-instances --filters Name=tag:Environment,Values=staging --query 'Reservations[*].Instances[*].[InstanceId,Tags]'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify changes are tested prior to deployment to production

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement automated rollback capabilities in deployment pipelines.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Verify CloudFormation rollback: aws cloudformation describe-stacks --query 'Stacks[*].[StackName,RollbackConfiguration]'
- Enable CodeDeploy automatic rollback: aws deploy update-deployment-group --application-name <app> --deployment-group-name <group> --auto-rollback-configuration '{"enabled":true,"events":["DEPLOYMENT_FAILURE","DEPLOYMENT_STOP_ON_ALARM"]}'
- List RDS automated backups for database rollback: aws rds describe-db-instances --query 'DBInstances[*].[DBInstanceIdentifier,BackupRetentionPeriod]'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify rollback procedures exist and are tested

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity identifies, selects, and develops risk mitigation activities for risks arising from potential business disruptions, including vendor and business partner dependencies.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Document risk mitigation strategies in the risk register and implement technical controls.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store risk treatment plan: aws s3 cp risk-treatment-plan.xlsx s3://<policy-bucket>/risk/risk-treatment-plan.xlsx
- Review Security Hub compliance score: aws securityhub get-findings --filters '{"ComplianceStatus":[{"Value":"FAILED","Comparison":"EQUALS"}]}' --query 'Findings | length(@)'
- Enable AWS Backup for data protection: aws backup create-backup-plan --backup-plan '{"BackupPlanName":"soc2-backup","Rules":[{"RuleName":"daily-backup","TargetBackupVaultName":"Default","ScheduleExpression":"cron(0 5 ? * * *)","Lifecycle":{"DeleteAfterDays":90}}]}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify risk mitigation strategies are documented and implemented

controls.title: Vendor and Business Partner Risk Management

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity assesses and manages risks associated with vendors and business partners, including due diligence, contractual requirements, and ongoing monitoring.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Maintain a vendor risk management program with documented assessments and contractual security requirements.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store vendor management policy: aws s3 cp vendor-management-policy.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/policies/vendor-management-policy.pdf
- Store vendor risk assessments: aws s3 sync ./vendor-assessments/ s3://<policy-bucket>/risk/vendor-assessments/
- List third-party IAM roles (vendor access): aws iam list-roles --query 'Roles[?contains(AssumeRolePolicyDocument | to_string(@), `arn:aws:iam::`) && !contains(Arn, `aws-service-role`)].[RoleName,Description]'
- Review cross-account access: aws iam get-role --role-name <vendor-role> --query 'Role.AssumeRolePolicyDocument'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify vendor risk management program with due diligence and ongoing monitoring

controls.title: Availability Commitments and System Performance Monitoring

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity maintains, monitors, and evaluates current processing capacity and use of system components (infrastructure, data, and software) to manage capacity demand and to enable the implementation of additional capacity to help meet its availability commitments.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement capacity monitoring and auto-scaling to meet availability commitments.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List Auto Scaling groups: aws autoscaling describe-auto-scaling-groups --query 'AutoScalingGroups[*].[AutoScalingGroupName,MinSize,MaxSize,DesiredCapacity]'
- Create scaling policy: aws autoscaling put-scaling-policy --auto-scaling-group-name <asg> --policy-name cpu-scale-out --policy-type TargetTrackingScaling --target-tracking-configuration '{"TargetValue":70.0,"PredefinedMetricSpecification":{"PredefinedMetricType":"ASGAverageCPUUtilization"}}'
- Create CloudWatch alarms for capacity: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name high-cpu --namespace AWS/EC2 --metric-name CPUUtilization --statistic Average --period 300 --threshold 85 --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold --alarm-actions <sns-arn>
- Check RDS capacity: aws rds describe-db-instances --query 'DBInstances[*].[DBInstanceIdentifier,DBInstanceClass,AllocatedStorage]'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose:
- List VM scale sets: az vmss list --query '[].{Name:name,Capacity:sku.capacity}'
- Enable autoscale: az monitor autoscale create --resource-group <rg> --resource <vmss-id> --resource-type Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachineScaleSets --min-count 2 --max-count 10 --count 2

parts.name: remediation-gcp
parts.prose:
- List instance groups: gcloud compute instance-groups managed list
- Set autoscaling: gcloud compute instance-groups managed set-autoscaling <group> --max-num-replicas 10 --target-cpu-utilization 0.7 --zone <zone>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify capacity monitoring and auto-scaling is configured

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Document SLA commitments and implement uptime monitoring dashboards.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store SLA documentation: aws s3 cp sla-commitments.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/policies/sla-commitments.pdf
- Create CloudWatch dashboard: aws cloudwatch put-dashboard --dashboard-name availability-dashboard --dashboard-body '{"widgets":[{"type":"metric","properties":{"metrics":[["AWS/ELB","HealthyHostCount"],["AWS/ELB","UnHealthyHostCount"]],"period":300,"title":"Load Balancer Health"}}]}'
- Create Route 53 health check: aws route53 create-health-check --caller-reference <ref> --health-check-config '{"IPAddress":"<ip>","Port":443,"Type":"HTTPS","ResourcePath":"/health","RequestInterval":30,"FailureThreshold":3}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify SLA and availability targets are documented and monitored

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, implements, operates, approves, maintains, and monitors environmental protections, software, data back-up processes, and recovery infrastructure to meet its objectives.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement automated backup with cross-region replication and regular recovery testing.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create backup plan: aws backup create-backup-plan --backup-plan '{"BackupPlanName":"soc2-backup","Rules":[{"RuleName":"daily","TargetBackupVaultName":"Default","ScheduleExpression":"cron(0 5 ? * * *)","StartWindowMinutes":60,"CompletionWindowMinutes":180,"Lifecycle":{"DeleteAfterDays":90},"CopyActions":[{"DestinationBackupVaultArn":"arn:aws:backup:<dr-region>:<account>:backup-vault:Default","Lifecycle":{"DeleteAfterDays":90}}]}]}'
- Assign resources to backup plan: aws backup create-backup-selection --backup-plan-id <plan-id> --backup-selection '{"SelectionName":"all-tagged","IamRoleArn":"<role-arn>","ListOfTags":[{"ConditionType":"STRINGEQUALS","ConditionKey":"Backup","ConditionValue":"true"}]}'
- List recovery points: aws backup list-recovery-points-by-backup-vault --backup-vault-name Default --query 'RecoveryPoints[*].[ResourceArn,CreationDate,Status]'
- Verify RDS automated backups: aws rds describe-db-instances --query 'DBInstances[*].[DBInstanceIdentifier,BackupRetentionPeriod,MultiAZ]'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose:
- Create Recovery Services vault: az backup vault create --name soc2-vault --resource-group <rg> --location <loc>
- Enable VM backup: az backup protection enable-for-vm --resource-group <rg> --vault-name soc2-vault --vm <vm-name> --policy-name DefaultPolicy

parts.name: remediation-gcp
parts.prose: - Create snapshot schedule: gcloud compute resource-policies create snapshot-schedule soc2-schedule --max-retention-days 90 --daily-schedule --start-time 04:00

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify backup and recovery procedures are implemented and tested

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Deploy critical systems across multiple availability zones or regions for resilience.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Check RDS Multi-AZ: aws rds describe-db-instances --query 'DBInstances[*].[DBInstanceIdentifier,MultiAZ,AvailabilityZone]'
- Enable Multi-AZ for RDS: aws rds modify-db-instance --db-instance-identifier <db-id> --multi-az --apply-immediately
- Check ELB cross-zone: aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[*].[LoadBalancerName,AvailabilityZones[*].ZoneName]'
- Verify ASG spans multiple AZs: aws autoscaling describe-auto-scaling-groups --query 'AutoScalingGroups[*].[AutoScalingGroupName,AvailabilityZones]'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify multi-AZ or multi-region deployment for critical systems

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity tests recovery plan procedures supporting system recovery to meet its objectives, including BCP and DR plan testing on at least an annual basis.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Maintain BCP/DR plans and conduct annual testing with documented results.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store BCP/DR plan: aws s3 cp bcp-dr-plan.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/policies/bcp-dr-plan.pdf
- Store DR test results: aws s3 cp dr-test-results-<date>.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/audits/dr-tests/
- Use AWS Resilience Hub for resilience assessment: aws resiliencehub create-app --name soc2-app --assessment-schedule Disabled
- Run resilience assessment: aws resiliencehub start-app-assessment --app-arn <app-arn> --app-version <version> --assessment-name annual-dr-test
- List assessment results: aws resiliencehub list-app-assessments --app-arn <app-arn>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify business continuity and disaster recovery plans are documented and tested

controls.title: Identification and Maintenance of Confidential Information

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity identifies and maintains confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality, including data classification, labeling, and handling procedures.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement data classification policy and use Macie for automated sensitive data discovery.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store data classification policy: aws s3 cp data-classification-policy.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/policies/data-classification-policy.pdf
- Enable Macie for sensitive data discovery: aws macie2 enable-macie
- Create classification job: aws macie2 create-classification-job --job-type ONE_TIME --s3-job-definition '{"bucketDefinitions":[{"accountId":"<account>","buckets":["<bucket>"]}]}' --name data-classification-scan
- List Macie findings: aws macie2 list-findings --finding-criteria '{"criterion":{"severity.description":{"eq":["High"]}}}'
- Tag S3 buckets with classification level: aws s3api put-bucket-tagging --bucket <bucket> --tagging 'TagSet=[{Key=DataClassification,Value=Confidential}]'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose:
- Create sensitivity labels: az security setting list
- Enable Azure Purview for data classification: az purview account create --name <account> --resource-group <rg>

parts.name: remediation-gcp
parts.prose:
- Enable DLP API: gcloud services enable dlp.googleapis.com
- Run DLP scan: gcloud dlp content inspect --content '<sample>' --info-types CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER EMAIL_ADDRESS

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify data classification policy and confidential data identification procedures exist

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable encryption at rest for all data stores containing confidential information.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Check S3 bucket encryption: aws s3api get-bucket-encryption --bucket <bucket>
- Enable default S3 encryption: aws s3api put-bucket-encryption --bucket <bucket> --server-side-encryption-configuration '{"Rules":[{"ApplyServerSideEncryptionByDefault":{"SSEAlgorithm":"aws:kms","KMSMasterKeyID":"<key-id>"},"BucketKeyEnabled":true}]}'
- Check RDS encryption: aws rds describe-db-instances --query 'DBInstances[?!StorageEncrypted].[DBInstanceIdentifier]'
- Check EBS encryption: aws ec2 describe-volumes --query 'Volumes[?!Encrypted].[VolumeId,State]'
- Enable default EBS encryption: aws ec2 enable-ebs-encryption-by-default
- Create Config rule: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule '{"ConfigRuleName":"encrypted-volumes","Source":{"Owner":"AWS","SourceIdentifier":"ENCRYPTED_VOLUMES"}}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify encryption at rest is enabled for all confidential data stores

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity disposes of confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality, including secure deletion, media sanitization, and key destruction.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement data retention and disposal policies with automated lifecycle management.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store data retention policy: aws s3 cp data-retention-policy.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/policies/data-retention-policy.pdf
- Configure S3 lifecycle rules for data expiration: aws s3api put-bucket-lifecycle-configuration --bucket <bucket> --lifecycle-configuration '{"Rules":[{"ID":"expire-confidential","Status":"Enabled","Filter":{"Tag":{"Key":"DataClassification","Value":"Confidential"}},"Expiration":{"Days":365}}]}'
- Schedule KMS key deletion for disposed data: aws kms schedule-key-deletion --key-id <key-id> --pending-window-in-days 30
- Verify no unencrypted snapshots: aws ec2 describe-snapshots --owner-ids self --query 'Snapshots[?!Encrypted].[SnapshotId,StartTime]'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify confidential data disposal procedures and retention policies

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity implements policies and procedures over system processing to result in products, services, and reporting to meet the entity's objectives related to processing integrity.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement input validation, API schema enforcement, and data integrity checks in application code.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable API Gateway request validation: aws apigateway update-rest-api --rest-api-id <api-id> --patch-operations op=replace,path=/minimumCompressionSize,value=0
- Create API Gateway request validator: aws apigateway create-request-validator --rest-api-id <api-id> --name validate-body --validate-request-body --validate-request-parameters
- Enable WAF SQL injection protection: aws wafv2 create-web-acl --name api-protection --scope REGIONAL --default-action '{"Allow":{}}' --rules '[{"Name":"SQLiProtection","Priority":1,"Statement":{"SqliMatchStatement":{"FieldToMatch":{"Body":{}},"TextTransformations":[{"Priority":0,"Type":"NONE"}]}},"Action":{"Block":{}},"VisibilityConfig":{"SampledRequestsEnabled":true,"CloudWatchMetricsEnabled":true,"MetricName":"sqli"}}]' --visibility-config '{"SampledRequestsEnabled":true,"CloudWatchMetricsEnabled":true,"MetricName":"api-waf"}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify input validation and data integrity controls are implemented

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity implements policies and procedures over system inputs to result in products, services, and reporting to meet the entity's objectives, ensuring completeness, accuracy, and timeliness.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement data validation rules, checksums, and reconciliation procedures for system inputs.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable DynamoDB stream for audit trail: aws dynamodb update-table --table-name <table> --stream-specification StreamEnabled=true,StreamViewType=NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES
- Create Lambda function for input validation: aws lambda create-function --function-name input-validator --runtime python3.12 --handler index.handler --role <role-arn> --zip-file fileb://validator.zip
- Set up dead-letter queue for failed validations: aws sqs create-queue --queue-name validation-failures-dlq

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify data input completeness and accuracy controls

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity implements policies and procedures over processing activities to result in products, services, and reporting to meet the entity's objectives, ensuring processing is accurate, complete, and timely.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Monitor processing latency and accuracy with CloudWatch metrics and alarms.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create custom CloudWatch metric for processing time: aws cloudwatch put-metric-data --namespace Application --metric-name ProcessingLatency --value <ms> --unit Milliseconds
- Create alarm for slow processing: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name slow-processing --namespace Application --metric-name ProcessingLatency --statistic Average --period 300 --threshold 5000 --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold --alarm-actions <sns-arn>
- Monitor Step Functions execution: aws stepfunctions list-executions --state-machine-arn <arn> --status-filter FAILED

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify processing monitoring and SLA tracking for timeliness

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity implements policies and procedures to make available or deliver output completely, accurately, and timely in accordance with specifications to meet the entity's objectives.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement output validation checksums and secure distribution channels.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable S3 object integrity checking: aws s3api put-object --bucket <bucket> --key <key> --body <file> --checksum-algorithm SHA256
- Verify object checksums: aws s3api head-object --bucket <bucket> --key <key> --checksum-mode ENABLED
- Create CloudWatch metric for output validation: aws cloudwatch put-metric-data --namespace Application --metric-name OutputValidationFailures --value 0 --unit Count
- Set up alarm: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name output-validation-failures --namespace Application --metric-name OutputValidationFailures --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 0 --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold --alarm-actions <sns-arn>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify output validation and distribution controls

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity implements policies and procedures to store inputs, items in processing, and outputs completely, accurately, and timely to meet the entity's objectives, including error handling and correction procedures.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement dead-letter queues, error logging, and automated retry mechanisms.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List SQS queues with DLQ: aws sqs list-queues --queue-name-prefix dlq
- Configure DLQ on Lambda: aws lambda update-function-configuration --function-name <func> --dead-letter-config '{"TargetArn":"arn:aws:sqs:<region>:<account>:<dlq-name>"}'
- Check Lambda error metrics: aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics --namespace AWS/Lambda --metric-name Errors --dimensions Name=FunctionName,Value=<func> --start-time <start> --end-time <end> --period 3600 --statistics Sum
- Create CloudWatch alarm for errors: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name lambda-errors --namespace AWS/Lambda --metric-name Errors --dimensions Name=FunctionName,Value=<func> --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --alarm-actions <sns-arn>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify error handling, dead-letter queues, and error correction procedures

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity provides notice to data subjects about its privacy practices to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy. The notice is updated and communicated to data subjects in a timely manner for changes to the entity's privacy practices.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Publish and maintain a privacy notice accessible to all data subjects.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store privacy notice: aws s3 cp privacy-notice.html s3://<website-bucket>/privacy-notice.html --content-type text/html
- Verify public accessibility via CloudFront: aws cloudfront list-distributions --query 'DistributionList.Items[*].[DomainName,Origins.Items[0].DomainName]'
- Store internal privacy policy: aws s3 cp privacy-policy.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/policies/privacy-policy.pdf
- Tag with review date: aws s3api put-object-tagging --bucket <policy-bucket> --key policies/privacy-policy.pdf --tagging 'TagSet=[{Key=ReviewDate,Value=<date>},{Key=Owner,Value=DPO}]'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify privacy notice exists, is current, and is accessible to data subjects

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity communicates choices available regarding the collection, use, retention, disclosure, and disposal of personal information to data subjects and obtains consent.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement consent management with documented opt-in/opt-out mechanisms.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create DynamoDB table for consent records: aws dynamodb create-table --table-name ConsentRecords --attribute-definitions AttributeName=user_id,AttributeType=S --key-schema AttributeName=user_id,KeyType=HASH --billing-mode PAY_PER_REQUEST
- Record consent: aws dynamodb put-item --table-name ConsentRecords --item '{"user_id":{"S":"<id>"},"consent_type":{"S":"marketing"},"granted":{"BOOL":true},"timestamp":{"S":"<iso-date>"},"ip_address":{"S":"<ip>"}}'
- Query consent status: aws dynamodb get-item --table-name ConsentRecords --key '{"user_id":{"S":"<id>"}}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify consent management mechanisms are implemented

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity collects personal information only for the purposes identified in the notice and limits collection to that which is necessary for the purposes identified.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Document data collection purposes and implement data minimization controls.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store data inventory: aws s3 cp data-inventory.xlsx s3://<policy-bucket>/privacy/data-inventory.xlsx
- Enable Macie for PII detection: aws macie2 enable-macie
- Create Macie classification job: aws macie2 create-classification-job --job-type ONE_TIME --s3-job-definition '{"bucketDefinitions":[{"accountId":"<account>","buckets":["<bucket>"]}]}' --name pii-discovery --managed-data-identifier-selector ALL
- Review findings: aws macie2 list-findings --sort-criteria '{"attributeName":"severity.score","orderBy":"DESC"}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify data collection is limited to documented purposes with data minimization

controls.title: Use, Retention, and Disposal of Personal Information

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity limits the use of personal information to the purposes identified in the notice and retains it only for as long as necessary.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement data retention schedules with automated disposal mechanisms.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store retention schedule: aws s3 cp data-retention-schedule.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/privacy/data-retention-schedule.pdf
- Configure DynamoDB TTL for automatic expiration: aws dynamodb update-time-to-live --table-name <table> --time-to-live-specification Enabled=true,AttributeName=ttl
- Configure S3 lifecycle for PII data expiration: aws s3api put-bucket-lifecycle-configuration --bucket <pii-bucket> --lifecycle-configuration '{"Rules":[{"ID":"expire-pii","Status":"Enabled","Filter":{"Prefix":"pii/"},"Expiration":{"Days":730}}]}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify data retention schedules and automated disposal for personal information

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity grants identified and authenticated data subjects the ability to access their stored personal information for review and, upon request, provides physical or electronic copies.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement DSAR procedures with automated data retrieval capabilities.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store DSAR procedure: aws s3 cp dsar-procedure.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/privacy/dsar-procedure.pdf
- Create DSAR tracking table: aws dynamodb create-table --table-name DSARRequests --attribute-definitions AttributeName=request_id,AttributeType=S --key-schema AttributeName=request_id,KeyType=HASH --billing-mode PAY_PER_REQUEST
- Track DSAR: aws dynamodb put-item --table-name DSARRequests --item '{"request_id":{"S":"<id>"},"requester":{"S":"<email>"},"status":{"S":"pending"},"requested_date":{"S":"<date>"},"due_date":{"S":"<date+30>"}}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify data subject access request (DSAR) procedures are documented and operational

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity corrects, amends, or appends personal information based on information provided by data subjects and communicates such corrections to third parties as committed or required.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Document data correction procedures and maintain an audit trail of corrections.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store correction procedure: aws s3 cp data-correction-procedure.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/privacy/data-correction-procedure.pdf
- Enable DynamoDB streams for correction audit trail: aws dynamodb update-table --table-name <user-data-table> --stream-specification StreamEnabled=true,StreamViewType=NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES
- Archive stream to S3 via Kinesis: aws kinesis create-stream --stream-name data-corrections-audit --shard-count 1

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify data correction request procedures are documented and tracked

controls.title: Disclosure and Notification of Personal Information

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity discloses personal information to third parties with the consent of the data subject only for the purposes identified in the notice, and notifies data subjects in the event of a breach.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Maintain data processing agreements with third parties and document breach notification procedures.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store DPAs: aws s3 sync ./data-processing-agreements/ s3://<policy-bucket>/privacy/dpas/
- Store breach notification plan: aws s3 cp breach-notification-plan.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/privacy/breach-notification-plan.pdf
- Create SNS topic for breach notifications: aws sns create-topic --name breach-notification
- Subscribe legal and compliance team: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <topic-arn> --protocol email --notification-endpoint legal@<domain>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify third-party data sharing agreements and breach notification procedures

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The entity collects and maintains accurate, up-to-date, complete, and relevant personal information for the purposes identified in the notice.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement data quality controls including validation rules, deduplication, and accuracy checks.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create Glue data quality rules: aws glue create-data-quality-ruleset --name pii-quality-rules --ruleset '{"Rules":[{"Name":"email_format","Expression":"ColumnValues['email'] matches '[a-zA-Z0-9+_.-]+@[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+'"}]}' --target-table '{"TableName":"<table>","DatabaseName":"<db>"}'
- Run data quality evaluation: aws glue start-data-quality-ruleset-evaluation-run --data-source '{"GlueTable":{"DatabaseName":"<db>","TableName":"<table>"}}' --role <role-arn> --ruleset-names pii-quality-rules
- Check results: aws glue get-data-quality-result --result-id <result-id>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify data quality controls and validation procedures for personal information
